etymology.description: semantic 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic 奐(奐) (huàn) -> to change
etymology.interpretation: The character 換(換) (huàn, "to change") combines the semantic component 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") and the phonetic component 奐(奐) (huàn). The hand indicates action, and along with the sound from 奐(奐), the character conveys changing or exchanging through manual activity.
etymology.simplification: In the simplified form, 换(換), the component 奐(奐) was retained to keep the phonetic element, while the structure of the character was slightly altered to simplify the strokes.

examples.chinese: 我要换一个房间(我要換一個房間)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ yào huàn yī gè fángjiān.
examples.english: I want to change a room.

examples.chinese: 他换了工作(他換了工作)，现在在银行上班(現在在銀行上班)。
examples.pinyin: Tā huàn le gōngzuò, xiànzài zài yínháng shàngbān.
examples.english: He changed jobs and now works at a bank.

examples.chinese: 我们应该换一种思维方式(我們應該換一種思維方式)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yīnggāi huàn yī zhǒng sīwéi fāngshì.
examples.english: We should change our way of thinking.
